API Reference

This page provides complete API documentation for all public classes and functions in StateCounter, automatically generated from source code docstrings.

Core Classes

Counter

The main class for creating and composing counters.

class statecounter.Counter(num_states=None, name=None, state=None, iter_order=None, *, _parents=None, _op=None)[source]

Bases: object

A counter that can be iterated and composed with other counters.

__init__(num_states=None, name=None, state=None, iter_order=None, *, _parents=None, _op=None)[source]

Create a counter.

property iter_order

Iteration order for this counter.

property num_states

Number of states this counter can take (read-only).

property id

Unique ID assigned by Manager (None if not registered).

property name

Name of this counter.

named(name)[source]

Set name and return self for fluent chaining.

property state

Current state of this counter.

advance()[source]

Advance to next state (wraps around).

reset(state=0)[source]

Reset to specified state (default 0).

is_active()[source]

Return True if counter is active (state is not None).

__iter__()[source]

Iterate through all states.

__getitem__(key)[source]

Create sliced counter: B = A[1:5] or A[::2] or A[::-1].

copy(name=None)[source]

Create a shallow copy with the same parents but a new Counter object.

deepcopy(name=None)[source]

Create a deep copy with all ancestors also copied.

print_dag(style='clean')[source]

Print the ASCII tree visualization rooted at this counter.

Parameters:

style (str) – Display style - ‘clean’ (default), ‘minimal’, or ‘repr’.

get_iteration_df(**kwargs)[source]
get_ancestors()[source]
get_states(include_inactive=True, named_only=True)[source]

Return dict of {name: state} for this counter and all ancestors.

Parameters:

  • include_inactive (bool) – If True (default), include counters with None state. If False, only include counters that are currently active.

  • named_only (bool) – If True (default), exclude counters with auto-generated names (Counter[N]). If False, include all counters.

Return type:

dict

Returns:

Dictionary mapping counter names to their current states, in reverse topological order (derived counters first, parents last).

print_states(include_inactive=True, named_only=True)[source]

Print current states of this counter and its ancestors.

Parameters:

  • include_inactive (bool) – If True (default), include counters with None state. If False, only include counters that are currently active.

  • named_only (bool) – If True (default), exclude counters with auto-generated names (Counter[N]). If False, include all counters.

Return type:

None

Manager

Context manager that must wrap all counter operations.

class statecounter.Manager[source]

Bases: object

Context manager for handling Counter objects.

__init__()[source]

Initialize empty counter manager.

__enter__()[source]

Enter context and set as active manager.

__exit__(exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb)[source]

Exit context and clear active manager.

register(counter)[source]

Register a counter with this manager.

get_ancestors(counter, visited=None)[source]

Recursively collect all ancestor counters in the computation graph.

inactivate_all(counters=None)[source]

Set counters to inactive state (None).

reset_all(counters=None)[source]

Reset counters to state 0.

get_all_names()[source]

Return list of names of all registered counters.

get_by_name(name)[source]

Return counter by name.

get_iteration_df(iter_counter, counters=None)[source]

Return DataFrame showing state of counters as iter_counter is iterated.

print_graph(style='clean')[source]

Print an ASCII tree visualization of the counter dependency graph.

Parameters:

style (str) – Display style - ‘clean’ (default), ‘minimal’, or ‘repr’.

Operation

Abstract base class for all counter operations.

class statecounter.Operation[source]

Bases: ABC

Base class for counter operations.

abstract compute_num_states(parent_num_states)[source]

Compute num_states for this node given parent num_states.

Return type:

int

abstract decompose(state, parent_num_states)[source]

Decompose this node’s state into parent states.

Return type:

tuple[int, ...]

Exceptions

exception statecounter.ConflictingStateAssignmentError[source]

Bases: RuntimeError

Raised when a counter receives conflicting state assignments during propagation.

Counter Operation Functions

These functions create new counters by combining or transforming existing counters.

Product Operations

statecounter.product(counters, name=None)[source]

Create a Counter representing the cartesian product of the provided Counters.

Parameters:

  • counters (Sequence[Counter_type]) – Sequence of parent Counters to combine into a product Counter. No duplicates allowed.

  • name (Optional[str], default=None) – Optional name for the resulting product Counter.

Returns:

A Counter whose states index the cartesian product of the input counters’ states.

Return type:

Counter_type

statecounter.ordered_product(counters, name=None)[source]

Create a product Counter from the provided counters, removing duplicates and automatically imposing an order based on counter.iter_order and counter.id.

This function recursively flattens nested product counters before deduplication, which handles diamond patterns where the same counter appears both as a direct parent and as an ancestor through another parent.

Parameters:

  • counters (Sequence[Counter_type]) – Sequence of parent Counters to combine into the product. Duplicates are removed and order is determined by (iter_order, id).

  • name (Optional[str], default=None) – Name for the resulting product Counter.

Returns:

A Counter representing the ordered, uniquified cartesian product of the input counters.

Return type:

Counter_type

Notes

Product is associative, so nested products are flattened: ordered_product([A*B, C, D*A]) becomes ordered_product([A, B, C, D])

Non-product operations (like stack, slice) are NOT flattened: ordered_product([stack(A,B), C]) keeps stack(A,B) as an atomic unit.

statecounter.set_product_order_mode(mode)[source]

Set the global ordering mode for ordered_product().

Return type:

None

statecounter.get_product_order_mode()[source]

Get the current global ordering mode for ordered_product().

Return type:

str

Stack (Disjoint Union)

statecounter.stack(counters, name=None)[source]

Create a Counter representing the disjoint union of the provided Counters.

Parameters:

  • counters (Sequence[Counter_type]) – Sequence of parent Counters to combine into a disjoint union Counter.

  • name (Optional[str], default=None) – Optional name for the resulting disjoint union Counter.

Returns:

A Counter whose states correspond to the disjoint union of the input Counters’ states.

Return type:

Counter_type

Synchronize

statecounter.sync(counters, name=None)[source]

Create a Counter that synchronizes the states of multiple parent Counters.

Parameters:

  • counters (Sequence[Counter_type]) – Sequence of parent Counters to synchronize.

  • name (Optional[str], default=None) – Optional name for the resulting synchronized Counter.

Returns:

A Counter whose states are the same as all parent Counters’ states.

Return type:

Counter_type

Slice

statecounter.slice(counter, start=None, stop=None, step=None, name=None)[source]

Create a Counter whose states correspond to a slice of the parent Counter.

Parameters:

  • counter (Counter_type) – The Counter to be sliced.

  • start (Optional[Integral], default=None) – Start index of the slice (inclusive).

  • stop (Optional[Integral], default=None) – Stop index of the slice (exclusive).

  • step (Optional[Integral], default=None) – Step size for the slice.

  • name (Optional[str], default=None) – Name for the resulting sliced Counter.

Returns:

A Counter whose states correspond to the specified slice of the parent Counter’s states.

Return type:

Counter_type

Repeat

statecounter.repeat(counter, times, name=None)[source]

Create a Counter that repeats the states of another Counter a specified number of times.

Parameters:

  • counter (Counter_type) – The Counter to repeat.

  • times (Integral) – Number of times to repeat the entire sequence of the parent’s states.

  • name (Optional[str], default=None) – Name for the resulting repeated Counter.

Returns:

A Counter whose states iterate through the parent’s states, repeated the specified number of times.

Return type:

Counter_type

Shuffle

statecounter.shuffle(counter, seed=None, permutation=None, name=None)[source]

Create a Counter with the states of a parent Counter randomly shuffled.

Parameters:

  • counter (Counter_type) – The Counter whose states will be shuffled.

  • seed (Optional[Integral], default=None) – Random seed to generate the shuffle permutation. Cannot be provided with ‘permutation’.

  • permutation (Optional[Sequence[Integral]], default=None) – Explicit permutation of parent state indices, as a sequence of length parent.num_values. Cannot be provided with ‘seed’.

  • name (Optional[str], default=None) – Name for the resulting shuffled Counter.

Returns:

A Counter whose state order corresponds to a permutation of the parent’s states.

Return type:

Counter_type

Sample

statecounter.sample(counter, num_states=None, sampled_states=None, seed=None, with_replacement=True, name=None)[source]

Create a Counter that samples states from the provided parent Counter.

Parameters:

  • counter (Counter_type) – Parent Counter whose states will be sampled.

  • num_states (Optional[Integral], default=None) – Number of states to sample from the parent. Mutually exclusive with ‘sampled_states’.

  • sampled_states (Optional[Sequence[Integral]], default=None) – Explicit sequence of parent state indices to use as samples. Mutually exclusive with ‘num_states’.

  • seed (Optional[Integral], default=None) – Random seed for sampling. Only relevant if sampling with ‘num_states’ and not supplying ‘sampled_states’.

  • with_replacement (bool, default=True) – Whether to sample with replacement (True) or without replacement (False).

  • name (Optional[str], default=None) – Name for the resulting sampled Counter.

Returns:

A Counter whose states are a sampled subset of those of the parent Counter.

Return type:

Counter_type

Split

statecounter.split(counter, split_spec, names=None)[source]

Split a Counter into multiple sub-Counters according to equal or proportional partitioning.

Parameters:

  • counter (Counter_type) – The Counter object to be split.

  • split_spec (Union[Integral, Sequence[Real]]) – If an integer N, split into N roughly equal parts. If a sequence of proportions, split according to these proportions (sequence length = number of parts).

  • names (Optional[Sequence[str]], default=None) – Optional sequence of names for each resulting sub-Counter. If provided, must match the number of parts.

Returns:

Tuple of new Counter objects corresponding to each partition of the original states.

Return type:

Tuple[Counter_type, …]

Interleave

statecounter.interleave(counters, name=None)[source]

Create a Counter that interleaves the states from multiple Counter objects.

Parameters:

  • counters (Sequence[Counter_type]) – Sequence of Counter instances to interleave, each with equal num_states.

  • name (Optional[str], default=None) – Optional name for the resulting interleaved Counter.

Returns:

A Counter whose states interleave across the provided counters.

Return type:

Counter_type

Passthrough

statecounter.passthrough(counter, name=None)[source]

Create a Counter that passes through its parent’s states unchanged.

Parameters:

  • counter (Counter_type) – Parent Counter whose states will be tracked.

  • name (Optional[str], default=None) – Optional name for the resulting passthrough Counter.

Returns:

A Counter that mirrors the states of its parent.

Return type:

Counter_type

Operation Classes

These are the underlying operation classes used internally. Most users will use the convenience functions above instead of instantiating these directly.

ProductOp

class statecounter.ProductOp[source]

Bases: Operation

Cartesian product of N counters.

compute_num_states(parent_num_states)[source]

Compute num_states for this node given parent num_states.

decompose(state, parent_num_states)[source]

Decompose this node’s state into parent states.

Bases: Operation

compute_num_states(parent_num_states)[source]

Compute num_states for this node given parent num_states.

decompose(state, parent_num_states)[source]

Decompose this node’s state into parent states.

SyncOp

class statecounter.SyncOp[source]

Bases: Operation

Keep N counters in lockstep.

compute_num_states(parent_num_states)[source]

Compute num_states for this node given parent num_states.

decompose(state, parent_num_states)[source]

Decompose this node’s state into parent states.

SliceOp

Bases: Operation

Slice a counter to select a subset of states.

Compute num_states for this node given parent num_states.

Repeat a single counter N times.

Compute num_states for this node given parent num_states.

Decompose this node’s state into parent states.

ShuffleOp

Randomly shuffle counter states using a deterministic seed.

Compute num_states for this node given parent num_states.

Sample states from parent counter.

Decompose this node’s state into parent states.
